title: HyperLog: Hybrid Small-Language-Model Pipeline for Cross-System Log Anomaly Detection with Calibrated Uncertainty
description:
HyperLog is a research-grade and production-grade hybrid encoder + projector + QLoRA-tuned compact decoder-only small language model pipeline for log anomaly detection. The release includes:

  • A faithful re-implementation of the LogLLM (Guan et al., 2024) baseline.
  • The HyperLog core: a sub-2B SLM decoder with QLoRA, in three training stages.
  • A strict zero-target-label leave-one-system-out (LOSO) cross-system evaluation protocol with mechanical SHA-256 leakage audits per fold.
  • Post-hoc temperature scaling calibration with reliability diagrams, ECE/MCE, AURC, Excess-AURC, and cost-asymmetric AURC.
  • Risk-coverage selective prediction with automatic tau selection.
  • Full eight-axis ablation matrix (A1-A8) with paired Wilcoxon + Holm-Bonferroni + Cliff's delta.
  • A FastAPI REST inference service (Phase 8) with API-key auth, per-key rate limiting, drift monitor, and committed OpenAPI spec.
  • One-command reproduction scripts for Windows and Linux.

Total test coverage at v1.0.0 is 420+ unit and integration tests.
keywords: deep learning for network and service management
notes: Reproducibility appendix: reports/phase9/reproducibility_appendix.md. Every numerical claim in the paper is backed by a JSON artefact under reports/.
